messages.author: swarm
messages.text:
What it is: The fake tmux is a real executable on PATH, so every tmux call in a test is a process spawn
What it solves: ⚠ NOT THE TICKET THE INTAKE THOUGHT IT WAS, EITHER. The intake listed `paneTailerClass`
(9.8s) as a long pole with a presumed "serial boot in one file" shape, and asked the
specifier to verify rather than assume. Verified: it is neither that, nor real timers —
BL-362 ALREADY replaced paneTailer's r…
How it works: An in-process caller's tmux call is served without spawning anything

messages.author: swarm
messages.text: BL-377 - The fake tmux is a real executable on PATH, so every tmux call in a test is a process spawn is complete.
